purpose: Operational map-art candidate inventory for SPLITROOT. This records free/premade assets already present locally, how they should be used at map scale, and the gameplay collision rules to prevent pretty meshes from creating invisible or misleading blockers.
live_canary_constraint: Do not close or restart the live SPLITROOT canary from this lane. Source changes become match-visible only after the refresh/build lane safely rebuilds and hot-loads between matches.

scale_reference.rule: Landmarks must be many player-heights tall, walkable/cover objects must match their gameplay role, and decorative edge dressing must stay outside primary routes and replay hot zones.

collision_policy.nonblocking_decor: DecorTree and DecorRock clusters are visual dressing only; collision disabled so they cannot trap players or squads.
collision_policy.cover_and_base_blockers: Cover stones and base/camp silhouettes may block, but only when placed as explicit gameplay cover/objectives and kept out of primary route centers.
collision_policy.resource_sites: ResourceSite meshes stay non-blocking. A player should be able to cross the visual footprint without being blocked by fountain or ruin geometry.
collision_policy.walkable_surfaces: Do not place walkable-looking meshes as non-walkable platforms until their collision and step-up behavior are tested in a rendered playtest.

map_read: Lenswright base moved from the old NE replay-hot cluster to the far east corner at 14500,5200.
NE route bends under the hot cluster instead of crossing it.
Existing free Fab assets are now used as trees, ice shelves, ruined stone, snow rock cover, resource-site visuals, and camp silhouettes.

candidates.title: English Oak Set
candidates.current_use: Deadwood west and southwest edge clusters in Splitroot Valley.
candidates.relative_scale: Use around 1.25-1.8 for tree dressing. Do not use as mountain or cover. The central Splitroot remains a separate many-player-height landmark.
candidates.risk: Can reduce route readability if allowed into lanes; keep pushed away from primary routes and replay hot zones.

candidates.title: Old Green/Red Wood Stumps
candidates.current_use: Central Splitroot and fallback tree silhouettes.
candidates.relative_scale: Only landmark usage should be enormous. Small stump usage must be visibly smaller than structures and outside traversal reads.
candidates.risk: Scaled-up stump collision must match visible trunk if used as a blocking landmark.

candidates.title: Wall of Ice
candidates.current_use: North ridge/shelf dressing and some ridge fallback visuals.
candidates.relative_scale: Use large enough to read as terrain from across the valley. Never smaller than player scale when presented as cliff, ridge, or ice wall.
candidates.risk: Complex collision on jagged ice is high-risk for invisible snagging; keep walkable routes away unless a rendered collision playtest proves it.

candidates.title: Castle Rocca Calascio Ruins
candidates.current_use: Lenswright ruin stones and fallback rock/cover silhouettes.
candidates.relative_scale: Use as medium-to-large ruin fragments. Avoid tiny fragments that read as pebbles beside the player.
candidates.collision_intent: nonblocking_decor unless promoted to explicit cover
candidates.risk: Ruin fragments look walkable; if collision blocks the player while visuals suggest a flat landing, demote to nonblocking decor or move out of traversal space.

candidates.title: Rock Terrain With Light Snow
candidates.current_use: Cover stones, Kinwild stonebreak, ridge fallback, and snow rock dressing.
candidates.relative_scale: Cover stones should stay roughly one to three player-heights tall; decorative rocks can be smaller but must not masquerade as mountains.
candidates.collision_intent: cover_and_base_blockers for CoverStone, nonblocking_decor for DecorRock
candidates.risk: The same asset family is used for both blocking cover and visual dressing; piece type must drive collision, not asset path.

candidates.title: Medieval Fountain Empty Free
candidates.relative_scale: Current resource-site footprint is wide for map readability but collision stays off so the visual surface does not block traversal.
candidates.risk: If upgraded into capture-zone architecture, add explicit walkable collision tests before enabling blocking.

candidates.title: FANTASTIC Village Pack
candidates.relative_scale: Use as base/camp structures, not small props. Keep settlement masses off route centers and away from player-spawn starts.
candidates.collision_intent: cover_and_base_blockers when used as base, otherwise nonblocking_decor
candidates.risk: Stylization may drift lighter than the AAA target; useful now for readable settlement mass, replace later if a higher-quality free pack is approved.

candidates.title: Large Medieval Tavern + Tavern Library
candidates.relative_scale: Large structures should read as buildings at many player-heights tall. Do not shrink to player-prop scale.
candidates.risk: The tavern library contains many component meshes; use curated picks only after visual verification to avoid clutter and collision mismatch.

candidates.title: Forest Ground Soil Pine
candidates.current_use: Not yet wired into the Valley ground plane.
candidates.relative_scale: Material only; tune tiling against the 36k x 24k valley so it does not look blurry or repetitive from FPS height.
candidates.risk: Needs material-slot verification in Unreal before claiming visual improvement.

candidates.title: Sacre Coeur + Torre d'en Galmes Sala Hipostila
candidates.current_use: Legacy Lenswright blockout reference only; not part of the current reseat pass.
recommended_map_roles: possible ruin POI after visual check
candidates.relative_scale: Only use as large landmarks if they fit SPLITROOT identity. Avoid recognizable real-world-monument dominance in the main playable lane.
candidates.risk: May be too recognizable, monolithic, or collision-heavy for the current map.

candidates.title: Free Windmill Village + Fantasy Architecture W2R16
recommended_map_roles: possible distant village read after visual check
candidates.relative_scale: Treat as unverified until rendered. Plane assets can look flat from FPS view and should not be used as core map identity without inspection.
candidates.risk: May be image-card/plane content rather than usable 3D set dressing.

needed_after_current_local_assets.reason: Current free village/tavern assets give readable camp mass but are not yet distinct enough for Verdant/Lenswright/Kinwild identity.
needed_after_current_local_assets.download_rule: Only add free assets with clear license/price evidence; send the URL before adding.

needed_after_current_local_assets.need: Walkable platform/bridge/ruin set with proven collision
needed_after_current_local_assets.reason: The user reported walkable-looking surfaces blocking incorrectly. We need assets whose visual surfaces and collision can be trusted or quickly simplified.
needed_after_current_local_assets.download_rule: Prefer free Unreal-native packs with collision documentation or simple modular meshes.

needed_after_current_local_assets.reason: Ground currently depends on debug material plus one pine-soil candidate that still needs tiling/render verification.
needed_after_current_local_assets.download_rule: Prefer free PBR terrain material packs with albedo/normal/roughness and UE material instances.
